title: INF-8 Self-Speculative UI knob (mode picker + draft-model picker + perf gain display)
goal: Self-Spec UI in Inference Lab per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES: mode picker (None / Ngram / DraftModel / Eagle3-deferred-badge), draft-model picker for DraftModel mode, live perf gain display (tokens/sec ratio + accept rate).
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES. SpeculativeDecodingPanel.svelte: mode select (None|Ngram|DraftModel); Ngram sub-controls (lookback, max_draft sliders); DraftModel sub-controls (model picker scoped to registry models with supportsSpeculativeDraft compatible + max_draft slider); Eagle3 entry visible but marked "deferred (llama.cpp PR #18039 pending)" with link to upstream tracking. Live perf-gain bar: subscribes to kernel_model_runtime_spec_stats (polls 2s); shows accept_rate %, observed speedup x. Hidden when supportsSpeculativeDraft=false. A/B compare: pick prompt, generate with spec on/off, show side-by-side latency.
risk_if_missed: INF-8 UI missing; operator cannot interactively tune speculative mode.
validator_focus: Eagle3 deferral surfaced (not hidden); draft-model picker scope correct (only compatible models); A/B compare flow present.

lifecycle.dependency_unblock_note: MT-109 deferred the Tauri IPC layer (commands/speculative.rs + lib.rs registration); MT-110 needs that IPC to function. Per operator 'work on dependencies first' rule the IPC was built as MT-110 unblock work and committed alongside the UI knob. Out-of-scope file additions: app/src-tauri/src/commands/speculative.rs (NEW, MT-109 deferred owned-file) + app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs (registration + SpeculativeModeOverrides.manage()) + app/src/lib/ipc/speculative.ts (NEW IPC binding). All carry operator-waiver-required notes consistent with MT-091/094 pattern.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Eagle3 deferral message clear (links to PR #18039).
Draft-model picker scope filtered by capability.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: app/src/components/inference_lab/SpeculativeDecodingPanel.tsx delivers the operator-facing mode picker with two of three MT-110 red_team minimum_controls satisfied and one partially-satisfied. SATISFIED: (1) Eagle3 deferral message clear (links to PR #18039) — the eagle3_deferred option is always present in the list when the option is visible, and the deferral note 'Eagle3 is deferred until llama.cpp PR #18039 merges and the adapter signals supports_eagle3=true (operator decision E-4)' is rendered as a paragraph below the picker with data-testid='speculative-decoding-panel.eagle3-deferred-note'. (2) Panel hidden when neither supportsSpeculativeDraft nor supportsEagle3 — explicit `if (panelHidden) return null` plus loop early-return. PARTIALLY SATISFIED: (3) Draft-model picker scope filtered by capability — the DraftModel branch in handleModeChange explicitly bails with error 'DraftModel picker UX is a follow-up MT — operator must bind a draft model id first.' — the draft-model picker UX itself is NOT implemented, only acknowledged as deferred. NOT SATISFIED: A/B compare latency display — the contract calls for 'A/B compare: pick prompt, generate with spec on/off, show side-by-side latency'; the implementation has no generate-on/off flow at all (no kernel_model_runtime_spec_stats poll subscription either). The Tauri IPC layer (commands/speculative.rs) was built as MT-110 unblock work for MT-109's deferred scope, which is honestly documented in dependency_unblock_note. Operator stance ('don't fail on paperwork; care about technique correctness') — the Eagle3 deferral handling is exemplary; the draft-model + A/B-compare gaps are real product-feature debt the MT explicitly recorded.
hbr_unsatisfied: HBR-INT-002 wire-evidence — SPEC-MODE-CHANGE FR event is declared at the technique surface (MT-109) but not emitted from the Tauri command surface; the commit path does not write a typed FEMS record.
